maxLimit.value: 1.5 months' rent (a tenant may voluntarily pay more).
sources: LawDistrict: Security Deposit Laws by State

capSummary.notes: Arizona requires the late fee to be reasonable and stated in the lease; no fixed percentage cap.

maxLimit.value: One month's rent (AB 12, effective July 1, 2024). A small landlord who owns no more than 2 residential properties with 4 or fewer total units may collect up to two months' rent.
sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits

rentIncreaseDays.notes: AB 1482 caps annual increases at 5% + local CPI (max 10%) for most units more than 15 years old. Many cities (LA, SF, Oakland) have stricter local control.

capSummary.notes: California has no statutory percentage cap, but a late fee must be a reasonable pre-estimate of the landlord's actual damages. Courts often treat fees above ~5–6% of rent as suspect.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Colorado requires 60 days for some increases under recent law — verify.

capSummary.notes: Colorado (2021): late fee capped at the greater of $50 or 5% of past-due rent, after a 7-day grace period.

interestSummary.value: Interest is paid at the deposit-index rate the CT Banking Commissioner sets each year. The 2026 deposit index is 0.49% — confirm the current year's rate before relying on it.

capSummary.notes: Connecticut caps the late fee at the lesser of $50 or 5% of the delinquent rent (the $5/day rule also tops out at $50), after a 9-day grace period (4 days for week-to-week). Only one late charge per payment.
Delaware: 5% of the monthly rent, chargeable after a 5-day grace period (longer if the landlord has no in-county rental office).

interestSummary.value: Deposits earn interest at no less than the statement-savings rate prevailing on January 1 and July 1 for each 6-month period of the tenancy, paid when the tenancy ends. The prefilled 1.5% is an estimate — confirm the actual prevailing rate.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Much of DC's housing is rent-controlled; controlled units have separate annual adjustment limits and longer notice.

capSummary.notes: DC caps late fees at 5% of monthly rent with a 5-day grace period.

maxLimit.value: No statutory limit on the deposit amount.
sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits

interestSummary.value: Interest is only owed if the landlord holds the deposit in an interest-bearing account. If so, the tenant receives at least 75% of the account's annualized average rate OR 5% simple interest — the landlord's choice. No interest is owed if the deposit is in a non-interest account or covered by a surety bond.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Florida has no statute setting a specific rent-increase notice period for month-to-month tenancies beyond the 15-day termination rule; 30 days is common practice. Some counties have added notice rules.

terminationDays.notes: Notice must be given before the end of the monthly period.

capSummary.notes: Florida has no statutory cap; the late fee must be in the lease and reasonable.
Hawaii caps late fees at 8% of the rent due; no statutory grace period.

maxLimit.value: No statewide limit. Some cities (e.g., Chicago) impose their own rules.
sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits
LawDistrict: Security Deposit Laws by State

interestSummary.value: In buildings of 25+ units, deposits held six months or more earn interest at the rate the largest IL commercial bank pays on minimum passbook savings as of Dec 31 (the IDFPR publishes it; the 2026 rate is about 0.01% APY).

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Statewide rent control is preempted; Chicago has notice ordinances.

terminationDays.notes: Chicago RLTO adds its own requirements for covered units.

capSummary.notes: No statewide cap. Chicago's RLTO caps late fees at $10/month on the first $500 of rent plus 5% of any amount above $500.

interestSummary.value: Any interest earned on the deposit during the first five years of the tenancy belongs to the landlord. After five years, accrued interest is paid to the tenant.

capSummary.notes: Iowa sets per-day caps tied to rent: if rent is $700 or less, up to $12/day (max $40/month); if over $700, up to $20/day (max $100/month). Use those limits — this calculator shows no fixed percentage.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Portland and a few municipalities have local rent control.

capSummary.notes: Maine caps late fees at 4% of the monthly rent and requires a 15-day grace period — the strictest in the country.

interestSummary.value: Deposits of $50 or more earn simple interest at the greater of 1.5% per year or the 1-year U.S. Treasury (Constant Maturity) yield set each January, accruing monthly, payable only if held at least 6 months. The DHCD publishes the rate and a calculator.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Some Maryland counties (e.g. Montgomery, Prince George's) have rent stabilization.

capSummary.notes: Maryland caps late fees at 5% of the rent due. A 2025 law (HB 273, eff. Oct 1, 2025) adds grace periods that vary by county (e.g. 10 days in Montgomery, 5 days in Prince George's) — check your county.

sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits

interestSummary.value: Landlords must pay 5% per year (or the actual rate received from the bank, if lower) on deposits held for at least one year.

capSummary.notes: Massachusetts prohibits charging a late fee until the rent is 30 days late.

sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits
LawDistrict: Security Deposit Laws by State

terminationDays.notes: 1 month's notice (equal to the rental interval)

interestSummary.value: Landlords must pay simple, non-compounded interest at 1% per year (since Aug 1, 2003) on the full deposit.

capSummary.notes: Minnesota caps late fees at 8% of the overdue rent.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Nevada requires 60 days for month-to-month and 45 days for periodic tenancies under 1 month — verify which applies.

capSummary.notes: Nevada caps late fees at 5% of the periodic rent, and no fee may be charged until at least 3 days after rent is due (for tenancies longer than week-to-week).

interestSummary.value: Deposits held for one year or more earn interest at the regular savings rate paid by the NH bank, savings & loan, or credit union holding the deposit.

sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits
LawDistrict: Security Deposit Laws by State

interestSummary.value: Deposits must be held in an interest-bearing account; the tenant receives the interest the account actually earns (an insured money-market/savings rate for landlords with 10+ units). Failing to comply lets the tenant claim 7% per year.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Over 100 NJ municipalities have local rent control with their own caps and notice rules.

capSummary.notes: New Jersey requires a 5-business-day grace period for senior and lower-income tenants; no fixed percentage cap.

interestSummary.value: On a lease of at least one year, if the deposit exceeds one month's rent, the landlord must pay annual interest at the passbook savings rate on the portion above one month's rent.

capSummary.notes: New Mexico caps late fees at 10% of the rent for the period.

maxLimit.value: One month's rent (since the 2019 Housing Stability and Tenant Protection Act).
sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits

interestSummary.value: In buildings of 6+ units, deposits must be placed in an interest-bearing NY bank account; the tenant gets the interest minus a 1% annual administrative fee the landlord may keep.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: NYC and many localities have rent stabilization with separate increase limits set by Rent Guidelines Boards.

capSummary.notes: New York caps late fees at the lesser of $50 or 5% of the monthly rent, after a 5-day grace period.
North Carolina caps the late fee at the greater of $15 or 5% of the monthly rent, after a 5-day grace period.

interestSummary.value: The deposit must be held in a federally insured interest-bearing account. For tenancies of nine months or more, the landlord pays simple interest at the Federal Reserve discount rate as of January 1 — confirm the current rate.
Deposits over $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) earn 5% per year on the excess, once the tenant has occupied the unit for six months, paid annually.

terminationDays.notes: 30 days' notice before the periodic rental date

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Oregon caps annual increases (SB 608) at 7% + CPI, capped at 10%. No increase is allowed during the first year of a tenancy; buildings under 15 years old are exempt.

terminationDays.notes: After the first year, Oregon generally requires cause (ORS 90.427); do not rely on a bare 30-day no-cause notice past year one.

capSummary.notes: Oregon allows a late fee only after a 4-day grace period; it must be a reasonable flat fee or a reasonable daily/percentage charge.

maxLimit.value: Up to 2 months' rent in the first year; no more than 1 month's rent thereafter.
sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits

interestSummary.value: For deposits over $100, once held more than two years the tenant earns the escrow account's interest rate on the full deposit, minus a 1% fee the landlord may retain.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Rhode Island requires 30 days, or 60 days if the tenant is 62 or older.

terminationDays.notes: 1 month's notice from the landlord (tenant owes only 15 days)

capSummary.notes: Tennessee caps late fees at 10% of the past-due rent, after a 5-day grace period.

maxLimit.value: No statutory limit on the deposit amount.
maxLimit.statute: Tex. Prop. Code ch. 92 (no limit specified)
sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits

rentIncreaseDays.statute: No specific Texas statute (governed by the lease/rental period)
rentIncreaseDays.notes: Texas has no statute mandating advance notice of a rent increase; for month-to-month, give notice at least one rental period (commonly 30 days) ahead, or as the lease requires.

terminationDays.notes: 1 month's notice (tenancy ends the later of the notice date or one month after notice)

capSummary.notes: Texas allows a late fee after a 2-day grace period; it is presumed reasonable if it does not exceed 12% of rent (units in buildings of 4 or fewer) or 10% (larger buildings).

terminationDays.notes: 9 V.S.A. § 4467 tiers the notice by length of occupancy; some municipalities add just-cause rules.

sources: Nolo: State Laws on Security Deposit Limits
LawDistrict: Security Deposit Laws by State

terminationDays.notes: 30 days' written notice before the next rent due date

capSummary.notes: Virginia caps late fees at the lesser of 10% of the periodic rent or 10% of the remaining balance owed; rent is late if unpaid by the 5th.

rentIncreaseDays.notes: Washington's 2025 law (HB 1217) caps most annual increases (around 7% + CPI, max 10%) and requires written notice — verify the current cap and notice period, which were recently revised.

capSummary.notes: Wisconsin requires the late fee to be specified in the lease; no fixed cap.
